articles.title: SSPDF, UPDF clash over Kajo-Keji border dispute
articles.summary: Clashes erupted between the South Sudan People’s Defence Forces (SSPDF) and the Uganda People’s Defence The post SSPDF, UPDF clash over Kajo-Keji border dispute appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The Kajo-Keji border dispute has historical roots in the post-independence tensions between South Sudan and Uganda, exacerbated by the 2013 civil war in South Sudan that destabilized the region. Recent escalations have been fueled by the influx of refugees and the competition for resources, particularly in the context of the 2020 peace agreement aimed at stabilizing South Sudan. The ongoing clashes threaten to undermine regional security and diplomatic relations between the two nations.

articles.title: South Sudan, Kenya deepen Cooperation on Border Security and Immigration
articles.summary: South Sudan and Kenya have reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening cooperation on border security and immigration management following high-level discussions held in Nairobi. According to Kenya media reports, the talks took place during a courtesy meeting between South Sudan’s Minister of Interior, Aleu Ayieny Aleu, and his Kenyan counterpart, and Kenya’s Cabinet Secretary for Interior and […] The post South Sudan, Kenya deepen Cooperation on Border Security and Immigration appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The border between South Sudan and Kenya has been a source of tension, particularly due to issues related to cattle rustling and cross-border crime. In 2012, both countries signed the Cooperation Agreement to enhance security and trade, but implementation has been inconsistent. The recent discussions in Nairobi come amid ongoing instability in South Sudan, where the peace process remains fragile, making effective border management crucial for both nations' security and economic interests.

articles.title: جوجل تحتفل بمرور 25 عامًا على Google Images بتصميم جديد وميزات ذكاء اصطناعي
articles.summary: أعلنت جوجل عن تحديث كبير لخدمة Google Images بالتزامن مع مرور 25 عامًا على إطلاقها، في خطوة تهدف إلى جعل تجربة البحث عن الصور أكثر سرعة وذكاءً، مع إضافة أدوات جديدة تعتمد على الذكاء الاصطناعي، من بينها إمكانية إنشاء الصور مباشرة من نتائج البحث. واجهة جديدة بنتائج أكثر ذكاءً رغم أن التصميم الجديد لا يختلف …
articles.context: تأتي هذه التحديثات من جوجل في وقت حساس بالنسبة للسودان، حيث يشهد البلد صراعًا مستمرًا منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. في ظل هذه الظروف، تزايد الاعتماد على التكنولوجيا والإنترنت كوسيلة للتواصل والمعلومات، مما يجعل تحسين أدوات البحث عن الصور أكثر أهمية للمستخدمين في المنطقة. كما أن الذكاء الاصطناعي قد يسهم في تعزيز الوصول إلى المعلومات في ظل انقطاع الخدمات.

articles.title: Kajo-Keji commissioner says Ugandan army attacked SSPDF post
articles.summary: Kajo-Keji County Commissioner Wani Jackson Mule has accused the Uganda People’s Defence Forces (UPDF) of crossing into South Sudan territory and attacking a South Sudan People’s Defence Forces (SSPDF) base along the border in Kajo-Keji County. Mule said the clashes occurred on Tuesday evening in the border area, forcing residents to flee their homes and […] The post Kajo-Keji commissioner says Ugandan army attacked SSPDF post appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Kajo-Keji region has been a flashpoint for tensions between Uganda and South Sudan, particularly since the 2011 independence of South Sudan, which has seen sporadic border skirmishes. The 2012 agreement between the two countries aimed to enhance security cooperation, yet violations have persisted. Recent clashes, like the one reported by Commissioner Mule, highlight the fragility of this relationship amid ongoing instability in South Sudan, exacerbated by internal conflicts and the presence of armed groups.

articles.title: محمود غالي يطرح كليب الحلاوة دي.. وهالة صدقي تظهر بتقنية الذكاء الاصطناعي
articles.summary: طرح المطرب الشاب محمود غالي أحدث كليباته الغنائية بعنوان “الحلاوة دي” عبر موقع “يوتيوب”، في عمل يجمع بين الأجواء الصيفية المبهجة والمعالجة البصرية الحديثة. وشهد الكليب ظهور الفنانة هالة صدقي كموديل باستخدام تقنيات الذكاء الاصطناعي، في فكرة لاقت إعجابها منذ اللحظة الأولى بمجرد عرضها عليها، خاصة في ظل علاقة الصداقة القوية التي تجمعها بالمطرب محمود …
articles.context: في ظل الأوضاع السياسية المتوترة في السودان، حيث يشهد البلد صراعات داخلية منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023، يسعى الفنانون مثل محمود غالي إلى تقديم رسائل إيجابية من خلال أعمالهم الفنية. كما أن استخدام تقنيات الذكاء الاصطناعي في الفن يعكس التوجهات الحديثة في صناعة الترفيه، مما يعزز من قدرة الفنانين على الابتكار في ظل الظروف الصعبة.

articles.title: بدء تصحيح امتحانات الشهادة الابتدائية بولاية نهر النيل للعام 2026
articles.summary: بدأت بمدينة الدّامر اليوم عمليات تصحيح أوراق امتحانات الشهادة الابتدائية، بولاية نهر النيل للعام 2026، وذلك وبمشاركة (253) معلّماً ومعلّمة. وأشاد الأستاذ أحمد حامد أحمد يس المدير العام لوزارة التربية والتوجيه بولاية نهر النيل الوزير المُكَلّف بالجهود الكبيرة التي بذلها المعلّمون، والمعلّمات، خلال العام الدراسي المنصرم، في ظل ظروف استثنائية شهدتها البلاد وساهمت هذه الجهود …
articles.context: تأتي عمليات تصحيح امتحانات الشهادة الابتدائية في ولاية نهر النيل في ظل تحديات كبيرة تواجه النظام التعليمي في السودان، خاصة بعد النزاع المستمر منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع. هذا الصراع أثر بشكل كبير على التعليم والبنية التحتية، مما جعل جهود المعلمين في هذا العام الدراسي أكثر أهمية. كما أن الحكومة الانتقالية تسعى لتعزيز الاستقرار التعليمي كجزء من جهودها لإعادة بناء البلاد.

articles.title: Machar’s co-accused asks court to dismiss charges, says prosecution lacks evidence
articles.summary: A lawmaker standing trial alongside suspended First Vice President Riek Machar on Wednesday asked the The post Machar’s co-accused asks court to dismiss charges, says prosecution lacks evidence appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The trial of Riek Machar, South Sudan's suspended First Vice President, is set against the backdrop of a fragile peace agreement signed in 2018, which aimed to end years of civil war. Tensions remain high as Machar's political faction, the Sudan People's Liberation Movement in Opposition (SPLM-IO), grapples with accusations of undermining the transitional government. The ongoing legal proceedings reflect broader struggles for power and accountability in a country still healing from conflict.

articles.title: تعاون سوداني برازيلي في الزراعة والبحث العلمي ودعم صغار المزارعين
articles.summary: بحث وكيل وزارة الخارجية والتعاون الدولي السفير معاوية عثمان خالد، ضمن زيارته الرسمية للبرازيل، بالسفيرة لويزا دا سيلفا نائبة مدير الوكالة البرازيلية للتعاون الدولي ؛ سبل تعزيز التعاون الفني والتنموي بين البلدين، مع التركيز على المجالات الزراعية. واتفق الجانبان على ابتعاث خبراء وفنيين من وإلى السودان بهدف تحديد احتياجات السودان في مجال الأبحاث الزراعية وإعادة …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الزيارة في وقت حرج للسودان، حيث يعاني من أزمات اقتصادية وصراعات داخلية متزايدة منذ اندلاع النزاع في 2023 بين القوات المسلحة السودانية وقوات الدعم السريع. تسعى الحكومة السودانية إلى تعزيز التعاون الدولي، وخاصة مع البرازيل، لتعزيز الأمن الغذائي ودعم صغار المزارعين، مما يعكس أهمية الزراعة كقطاع حيوي في استقرار البلاد وتنميتها.

articles.title: تقييم أداء مشروع توفير خدمات الرعاية الصحية والتغذوية للمجتمعات الهشة بسنار
articles.summary: ناقش اجتماع المراجعة الدورية للربع الثالث لمشروع توفير خدمات الرعاية الصحية والتغذوية المتكاملة للمجتمعات الهشة بولاية سنار اليوم والذي تنفذه منظمة الرعاية الصحية الشاملة (بانكير) بالتنسيق مع وزارة الصحة وبدعم من البنك الدولي، ناقش التقدم المحرز خلال الربع الثالث من تنفيذ المشروع. وذلك بحضور مدير عام وزارة الصحة والتنمية الإجتماعية المناوب دكتور عوض الطيب أحمد …
articles.context: تأتي هذه المراجعة في وقت حساس حيث تواجه ولاية سنار تحديات كبيرة في مجال الرعاية الصحية بسبب النزاعات المستمرة والأزمات الاقتصادية. منذ اندلاع النزاع في السودان في 2023، زادت الحاجة إلى خدمات الرعاية الصحية والتغذوية، مما يجعل مشروع منظمة بانكير مدعومًا من البنك الدولي ذا أهمية خاصة لتلبية احتياجات المجتمعات الهشة. يهدف المشروع إلى تحسين الوضع الصحي في ظل الظروف الصعبة التي تمر بها البلاد.

articles.title: Nasir Trial: Third accused Gatwech concludes defense, denies role in Nasir attack
articles.summary: The third accused in the ongoing Special Court trial over the Nasir garrison attack, Gatwech Lam, has denied allegations that he supported, encouraged, or participated in activities linked to the 2025 Nasir crisis, asking the court to dismiss the case against him. Lam, a Member of Parliament, concluded his defense statement on Wednesday during the […] The post Nasir Trial: Third accused Gatwech concludes defense, denies role in Nasir attack appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Nasir garrison attack in 2025 was a pivotal event in South Sudan's ongoing conflict, exacerbating tensions between rival factions within the Sudan People's Liberation Army (SPLA). The trial of Gatwech Lam and others is part of broader efforts to address accountability following years of violence and political instability, particularly after the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which aimed to establish peace and governance reforms.

articles.title: وزير الشباب والرياضة يخاطب كورس حكام كرة القدم بجامعة السودان
articles.summary: خاطب البروفيسور أحمد آدم وزير الشباب والرياضة، اليوم فعاليات كورس حكام كرة القدم الذي تنظمه كلية التربية البدنية والرياضة بجامعة السودان للعلوم والتكنولوجيا، في إطار جهود الوزارة الرامية إلى نشر الثقافة الرياضية وتعزيز ممارسة الرياضة في أوساط الطلاب الجامعيين، باعتبارهم القاعدة الحقيقية لبناء القيادات الرياضية وصناعة مستقبل الرياضة السودانية. وأكد الوزير أن الجامعات تمثل بيئة …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الفعالية في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات سياسية واقتصادية كبيرة، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. تسعى وزارة الشباب والرياضة، بقيادة البروفيسور أحمد آدم، إلى تعزيز الاستقرار الاجتماعي من خلال الرياضة، حيث تعتبر الجامعات مركزًا لتكوين قيادات شابة قادرة على المساهمة في إعادة بناء البلاد.

articles.title: إدارة المياه والسدود بالقضارف تنفذ عدد من مشروعات المياه
articles.summary: كشف المهندس عبدالمنعم الضو مدير إدارة المياه والسدود بوزارة البنى التحتية والتنمية العمرانية بولاية القضارف عن استكمال انشاء ستة حفائر لتخزين المياه بجانب إعادة تأهيل سد السبعات الترابي ليسهم في تحويل المياه لعدد أربعة حفائر في منطقة قرى السبعات . وقال عبدالمنعم في تصريح إن إدارته ركزت هذا العام على المناطق الأكثر تأثرا بقلة المياه …
articles.context: تواجه ولاية القضارف في السودان تحديات كبيرة في إدارة المياه بسبب التغيرات المناخية والنزاعات المستمرة حول الموارد المائية. في عام 2021، شهدت المنطقة نقصًا حادًا في المياه نتيجة للجفاف، مما زاد من الحاجة إلى مشاريع تخزين المياه. تأتي هذه المبادرات في إطار جهود الحكومة المحلية لتعزيز الأمن المائي وتحسين الظروف المعيشية للسكان، خاصة في ظل الأزمات الاقتصادية والسياسية التي تعاني منها البلاد.

articles.title: كيف تحولت منصات التواصل الكبرى إلى بوابة للترويج لأدوات التزييف العميق؟
articles.summary: كشفت دراسة حديثة أجراها معهد الحوار الاستراتيجي (ISD) أن منصتي يوتيوب وX وجهتا ملايين المستخدمين إلى مواقع إلكترونية تقدم أدوات لتجريد الأشخاص من ملابسهم باستخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي (Nudify Tools) في الفترة ما بين ديسمبر 2025 ومارس 2026. وأشار التقرير إلى أن هاتين المنصتين تحولتا إلى مصدري حركة مرور رئيسيين لخدمات تنشئ صوراً حميمية دون رضا …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الدراسة في وقت حساس للسودان، حيث يشهد البلد صراعاً داخلياً متزايداً منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع. في ظل هذه الفوضى، تزايدت المخاوف بشأن استغلال التكنولوجيا، مثل أدوات التزييف العميق، في نشر المعلومات المضللة واستغلال الأفراد، مما يهدد الأمن الشخصي ويزيد من التوترات الاجتماعية في البلاد.

articles.title: انتصار فى يوم ميلادها.. رحلة من مسرح الجامعة إلى قلوب الملايين
articles.summary: تحتفل اليوم الفنانة انتصار بعيد ميلادها، المولودة في الإسكندرية لأسرة صعيدية، والتي بدأت مسيرتها الفنية عام 1991، وحملت معها شهادة بكالوريوس التجارة من جامعة الإسكندرية إلى عالم الأضواء، ولم تكن البداية مفاجئة، بل جاءت عبر المشاركات السينمائية والتلفزيونية، إضافة إلى عروض مسرحية في الجامعة ساهمت في صقل موهبتها، لتكوّن شهرة سريعة بأدوار ثانوية أثبتت من …
articles.context: تحتفل الفنانة انتصار بعيد ميلادها في وقت يتسم بتحديات كبيرة تواجه قطاع الفن في السودان، حيث يعاني من تأثيرات الصراع المستمر منذ عام 2019، والذي أثر على جميع جوانب الحياة بما في ذلك الثقافة والفنون. تأتي هذه الاحتفالية في ظل جهود الحكومة الانتقالية لتعزيز الهوية الثقافية والفنية في البلاد، وسط محاولات لإعادة بناء المجتمع بعد سنوات من النزاع.

articles.title: W. Equatoria residents alarmed as fuel price surge drives up transport costs
articles.summary: Residents, commercial motorcycle taxi operators known as boda-boda riders, and farmers in Western Equatoria State The post W. Equatoria residents alarmed as fuel price surge drives up transport costs appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: Western Equatoria State has faced ongoing economic challenges exacerbated by the civil conflict in South Sudan, which began in December 2013. The recent surge in fuel prices, attributed to global market fluctuations and local supply chain disruptions, has significantly impacted transport costs, affecting livelihoods. This situation is particularly pressing as the region grapples with food insecurity and the aftermath of the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, which aimed to stabilize the country but has seen limited implementation.

articles.title: NPA supports ethical election reporting training in Bor
articles.summary: As South Sudan prepares for its first-ever general elections since gaining independence in 2011, the The post NPA supports ethical election reporting training in Bor appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: South Sudan is gearing up for its first general elections since independence in 2011, a significant milestone following years of civil conflict that erupted in 2013 and again in 2016. The peace agreement signed in 2018, which aimed to end the violence and establish a transitional government, has set the stage for these elections. Ethical reporting is crucial as the country navigates a fragile political landscape marked by mistrust and ongoing tensions among various factions.

articles.title: Sudan’s war economy fueling conflict, risks global supply chains: UN report
articles.summary: Report warns gum arabic trade linked to human rights abuses, urges stronger scrutiny of conflict-affected value chains
articles.context: Sudan has been embroiled in conflict since the Darfur crisis began in 2003, leading to widespread human rights abuses and the rise of armed groups. The 2021 military coup, which ousted the transitional government, exacerbated tensions, with factions vying for control over lucrative resources like gum arabic. The UN report highlights how these dynamics not only fuel local violence but also threaten global supply chains, particularly as Sudan is a major supplier of gum arabic used in food and cosmetics.

articles.title: كأس العالم: فيفا يفتح الباب أمام بطولة تضم 64 منتخباً، فما التحديات التي قد تواجه مستضيفها؟
articles.summary: من المقرر تقييم خطط إقامة كأس العالم لكرة القدم للرجال بمشاركة 64 منتخباً بعد نسخة 2026، إذ قال رئيس الاتحاد الدولي لكرة القدم (فيفا)، جياني إنفانتينو، إن البطولة يجب أن تكون “للعالم بأسره”. وكان مقترح توسيع البطولة قد طُرح العام الماضي، ويقول إنفانتينو إن نجاح البطولة الموسعة التي تضم 48 منتخباً يعني أن على فيفا …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطط في ظل التوترات السياسية والاقتصادية في منطقة القرن الأفريقي، حيث تواجه دول مثل السودان تحديات كبيرة بسبب النزاعات الداخلية والأزمات الإنسانية. في 2021، شهد السودان احتجاجات واسعة ضد الحكومة الانتقالية، مما أثر على استقراره. توسيع كأس العالم قد يوفر فرصة لتعزيز التعاون الإقليمي، لكن يتطلب استثمارات كبيرة في البنية التحتية، وهو ما قد يكون صعبًا في ظل الظروف الحالية.

articles.title: Fuel prices soar as pound plunges, South Sudanese feel economic pressure
articles.summary: South Sudanese residents have expressed concern over rising fuel prices and the continued depreciation of the South Sudanese pound, saying the increasing cost of living is putting pressure on households and businesses. Speaking during Eye Radio’s Dawn Show on Wednesday, callers from Juba, Wau, and other parts of the country said the exchange rate on […] The post Fuel prices soar as pound plunges, South Sudanese feel economic pressure appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The economic crisis in South Sudan has been exacerbated by ongoing conflict and political instability since the civil war erupted in December 2013, leading to a fractured economy and reliance on oil revenues. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to restore peace but has struggled to stabilize the currency, with the South Sudanese pound losing significant value against the US dollar, further inflating fuel prices and deepening the economic strain on citizens.

articles.title: Abyei people cannot remain hostages of Juba-Khartoum dispute -Yakani
articles.summary: Civil society activist Edmond Yakani has called on the governments of South Sudan and Sudan to resolve the long-standing dispute over the Abyei Administrative Area through peaceful dialogue, warning that residents of the area have remained caught in political delays. Yakani, the Executive Director of the Community Empowerment for Progress Organization (CEPO), said the continued […] The post Abyei people cannot remain hostages of Juba-Khartoum dispute -Yakani appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Abyei Administrative Area, claimed by both Sudan and South Sudan, has been a flashpoint since South Sudan's independence in 2011. The 2005 Comprehensive Peace Agreement (CPA) stipulated a referendum for Abyei's status, which has yet to occur due to disputes over voter eligibility and territorial claims. Recent tensions have escalated, with both governments failing to engage in meaningful dialogue, leaving the local population vulnerable amidst ongoing political stalemates.

articles.title: SSPDF recaptures Lankien, Nyirol County
articles.summary: The South Sudan People’s Defence Forces (SSPDF) have recaptured the town of Lankien in Nyirol The post SSPDF recaptures Lankien, Nyirol County appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The recapture of Lankien is significant as it reflects ongoing tensions between the SSPDF and opposition forces, particularly the Sudan People's Liberation Army-In Opposition (SPLA-IO) led by Riek Machar. This conflict has roots in the civil war that erupted in December 2013, exacerbated by the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, which has struggled to fully implement key provisions, including disarmament and power-sharing, leading to continued violence in regions like Nyirol County.

articles.title: Wau Rotary Club donates four rickshaw ambulances to Twic County
articles.summary: The Rotary Club of Wau, in partnership with the MAMA Project and other partners, has donated four customized motor tricycle ambulances to Transformation of the World in Christ (TWIC) to improve emergency healthcare access for remote and displaced communities in Twic County, Warrap State. The tuk-tuk ambulances are expected to provide affordable emergency transport for […] The post Wau Rotary Club donates four rickshaw ambulances to Twic County appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: Twic County, part of Warrap State in South Sudan, has faced ongoing humanitarian challenges due to years of civil conflict, which escalated in 2013 and again in 2016, leading to widespread displacement and inadequate healthcare access. The recent revitalized peace agreement in 2018 aimed to stabilize the region, but many communities remain underserved. The donation of rickshaw ambulances by the Wau Rotary Club and partners is a critical step in addressing urgent healthcare needs in these vulnerable areas.

articles.title: Rastafarians in Kenya fail in bid to get cannabis legalised for religious purposes
articles.summary: The court acknowledged the need for a broader national debate on cannabis policy in Kenya.
articles.context: In 2020, Kenya's National Authority for the Campaign Against Alcohol and Drug Abuse reported a rise in cannabis use, prompting discussions on drug policy reform. The Rastafarian community, advocating for the religious use of cannabis, has faced legal challenges, culminating in a recent court ruling on October 10, 2023, which emphasized the need for a comprehensive national debate on cannabis legislation, reflecting broader societal tensions around drug use and religious freedoms in Kenya.

articles.title: Adil says SPLM will not entertain community-based recommendations for elective positions
articles.summary: The Chairperson of the Sudan People’s Liberation Movement (SPLM) in Central Equatoria State, Emmanuel Adil Anthony, has said recommendations based on community interests as it prepares for the 2026 general elections, stressing that appointments must follow party procedures and merit, in line with resolutions from the recent SPLM Leadership Summit. His bail is seen as a potential turning point for opposition politics in Uganda.

articles.title: “They Came to Kill Me, Not Arrest Me” – Muwanga Kivumbi Breaks Silence After Luzira Release, Re-Arrested Hours Later
articles.summary: National Unity Platform (NUP) Deputy President for Buganda, Muwanga Kivumbi, has made explosive claims following his release from The post “They Came to Kill Me, Not Arrest Me” – Muwanga Kivumbi Breaks Silence After Luzira Release, Re-Arrested Hours Later appeared first on The Nile Post .
articles.context: Muwanga Kivumbi's situation reflects the ongoing political tensions in Uganda, particularly following the controversial 2021 presidential elections that saw President Yoweri Museveni extend his 37-year rule amid allegations of electoral fraud and repression of opposition figures. The National Unity Platform (NUP), led by Robert Kyagulanyi (Bobi Wine), has faced significant crackdowns, with Kivumbi's claims of attempted assassination highlighting the escalating risks for political dissenters in a climate marked by violence and human rights abuses.

articles.title: Wanyonyi breaks 1,000m world record on debut in Monaco
articles.summary: Kenya's Emmanuel Wanyonyi breaks the 27-year-old men's 1,000m world record at the Diamond League meeting in Monaco, and Julien Alfred runs a stunning 200m.
articles.context: Emmanuel Wanyonyi's record-breaking performance comes at a time when Kenya is striving to reclaim its dominance in middle-distance running, following a dip in international success in recent years. The country has faced challenges, including political unrest and the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sports events. Wanyonyi's achievement at the Diamond League in Monaco on July 21, 2023, symbolizes a potential resurgence for Kenyan athletics on the global stage.

articles.title: Kenya Power to Pay 15.5 Cents per kWh in Newly Finalized Ethiopia Energy Deal
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Electric Utility (EEU) has formalized a landmark power supply agreement with the Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC), marking a significant milestone in cross-border energy trade and regional infrastructure integration. Under the newly ratified framework, Kenya’s primary electricity distributor will purchase power from Ethiopia at a tariff 15.5 U.S. cents—per kilowatt-hour (kWh). In […]
articles.context: The power supply agreement comes amid ongoing efforts to enhance regional cooperation following the 2018 peace deal between Ethiopia and Kenya, which aimed to strengthen economic ties. The deal also reflects Ethiopia's ambition to become a regional energy hub, particularly after the completion of the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam in 2022. This agreement is crucial as both countries seek to stabilize their economies and address energy shortages, particularly in Kenya.

articles.title: Sudan ties acceptance of US peace roadmap to RSF withdrawal from all cities
articles.summary: Government document proposes 90-day humanitarian truce, UN-monitored RSF pullout before indirect ceasefire talks, 2 senior Sudanese officials tell Anadolu
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan escalated in April 2023 when clashes erupted between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), leading to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. The proposed US peace roadmap, which includes a 90-day humanitarian truce and UN-monitored RSF withdrawal, aims to facilitate indirect ceasefire talks, reflecting international efforts to stabilize the region amid a power struggle following the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019.

articles.title: Addis Ababa enacts 5% tax on hotel and lodging accommodations
articles.summary: The Addis Ababa City Council has ratified a new revenue-generating regulation tailored to the city’s rapidly expanding hospitality and tourism sector. Under Regulation No. 204/2026, a 5 percent flat municipal tax has been imposed on all hotel and lodging establishments operating within the capital. Published in the Negarit Gazeta, the regulation introduces several new provisions […]
articles.context: The introduction of the 5% tax on hotel and lodging accommodations comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from the Tigray conflict, which has significantly impacted the tourism sector. As the government seeks to boost revenue and stabilize the economy, this regulation aligns with the broader economic reforms initiated under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's administration since 2018, aimed at revitalizing the hospitality industry and attracting foreign investment post-conflict.

articles.title: Inside Uganda’s Biggest Cybercrime Bust: Over 1,000 Foreign Suspects Netted in High-Stakes Security Crackdown
articles.summary: A major security operation has shaken Uganda after authorities arrested more than 1,000 foreign nationals in an unprecedented The post Inside Uganda’s Biggest Cybercrime Bust: Over 1,000 Foreign Suspects Netted in High-Stakes Security Crackdown appeared first on The Nile Post .
articles.context: The cybercrime crackdown in Uganda comes amid rising tensions in the region, particularly following the 2021 elections that saw President Yoweri Museveni extend his rule amidst allegations of electoral fraud. Additionally, the ongoing conflict in South Sudan has led to increased cross-border crime, with foreign nationals often implicated in illicit activities. This operation reflects Uganda's intensified efforts to bolster national security and address the growing threat of cybercrime in a politically volatile environment.

articles.title: Speke Resort Convention Centre Lands Prestigious Global Partnership, Boosting Uganda’s International Conference Ambitions
articles.summary: Uganda’s meetings and events industry has received a significant boost after Speke Resort Convention Centre secured official partnership The post Speke Resort Convention Centre Lands Prestigious Global Partnership, Boosting Uganda’s International Conference Ambitions appeared first on The Nile Post .
articles.context: Uganda has been positioning itself as a regional hub for international conferences, especially following the 2020 East African Community (EAC) agreement aimed at enhancing trade and tourism. The ongoing instability in neighboring South Sudan, exacerbated by civil conflict since 2013, has increased the demand for secure venues in Uganda for regional meetings. This partnership with Speke Resort Convention Centre aligns with Uganda's strategic goals to attract international events amidst these regional challenges.

articles.title: Kenyan president says East Africa’s biggest refinery to move ahead in Lamu
articles.summary: Ruto says project backed by Nigerian billionaire Aliko Dangote will create 60,000 jobs, supply fuel across 8 African countries
articles.context: The Lamu Oil Refinery project, initially proposed in the 1970s, has faced numerous delays due to political instability and environmental concerns. Recently, President William Ruto's administration has prioritized infrastructure development to boost the economy, especially following the 2022 elections that saw a shift in power dynamics. The backing of Nigerian billionaire Aliko Dangote, known for his significant investments in Africa, adds a crucial financial component to this ambitious project, which aims to enhance regional fuel supply and create jobs.

articles.title: MELBET: INTO THE QUARTER-FINALS. Match previews and predictions
articles.summary: We’ll soon find out who the new champion is! The world’s best national teams keep fighting for the legendary trophy. In this article, MELBET assesses the teams’ chances of progressing to the next round. France vs Morocco, July 9 How can you stop this France side? It’s virtually impossible to beat them playing open football. […]
articles.context: The ongoing tensions in the Horn of Africa, particularly the conflict in Ethiopia's Tigray region, have overshadowed sports events, impacting national morale and unity. As Ethiopia grapples with internal strife since the Tigray War began in November 2020, the success of national teams in international tournaments like the MELBET can serve as a unifying force, offering a momentary escape and fostering national pride amidst the turmoil.

articles.title: المحكمة الجنائية الدولية تُبلغ بي بي سي بإحراز تقدم في التحقيق في جرائم الحرب بالسودان
articles.summary: تُجري المحكمة الجنائية الدولية تحقيقاً في الفظائع التي ارتُكبت في دارفور على مدى السنوات الثلاث الماضية.
articles.context: تعود جذور النزاع في دارفور إلى عام 2003، عندما اندلعت حرب أهلية بين الحكومة السودانية ومتمردين من القبائل غير العربية. في عام 2005، أصدرت المحكمة الجنائية الدولية مذكرات اعتقال بحق مسؤولين سودانيين، بما في ذلك الرئيس السابق عمر البشير، بتهم تتعلق بجرائم الحرب. مع استمرار التوترات السياسية بعد الإطاحة بالبشير في 2019، تكتسب تحقيقات المحكمة أهمية متزايدة في سياق الجهود لتحقيق العدالة والمساءلة.

articles.title: No Legal Meat Imports from Uganda to Ethiopia, Minister Announces Investigation
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Ministry of Agriculture (MoA) has formally denied issuing import permits for meat products from Uganda. In response to growing concerns over food safety regulatory breaches and illicit trade channels, the Ministry has launched an official investigation into how the unauthorized shipments entered the country. Fikru Regassa, State Minister for the Livestock and Fishery […]
articles.context: Ethiopia and Uganda have historically engaged in trade, but tensions have risen due to concerns over food safety and illicit trade practices. The 2020 signing of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement aimed to enhance regional trade, yet challenges persist, particularly in the livestock sector. The Ethiopian government's recent crackdown on unauthorized meat imports reflects ongoing efforts to safeguard public health amid rising scrutiny of cross-border trade dynamics.

articles.title: International court tells BBC of breakthrough in Sudan war crimes probe
articles.summary: The ICC has been looking into atrocities committed in Darfur over the past three years.
articles.context: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has been investigating war crimes in Sudan since 2005, focusing on the Darfur conflict that erupted in 2003, involving government forces and rebel groups like the Sudan Liberation Army. The recent escalation of violence following the 2021 military coup has exacerbated the humanitarian crisis, prompting renewed international attention and the ICC's efforts to hold perpetrators accountable, particularly in light of the ongoing power struggle between rival military factions.

articles.title: South Sudan Peace Deal Amendments Spark Concern
articles.summary: The embassies of Canada, France, Germany, Japan, the Netherlands, Norway, Switzerland, the United Kingdom and the United States, together with the European Union Delegation to South Sudan, issued a joint statement on Wednesday expressing grave concern over amendments made to the Peace Agreement adopted by South Sudan’s Transitional National Legislative Assembly (TNLA). The statement followed […]
articles.context: The amendments to the Peace Agreement, originally signed in September 2018, have raised alarms as they threaten the fragile stability established after years of civil war in South Sudan. The TNLA's recent changes, which critics argue undermine the power-sharing provisions, come amid ongoing tensions between President Salva Kiir and opposition leader Riek Machar, exacerbating fears of renewed conflict in a country still grappling with humanitarian crises and governance challenges.

articles.title: Anbesa Bank shareholders seek urgent meeting with NBE Governor after board election approval revoked
articles.summary: A one third shareholders and the leadership of Anbesa Bank have requested an urgent meeting with National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE) Governor Eyob Tekalign after the central bank revoked its earlier approval of the bank’s 21st General Assembly minutes related to the election of its board of directors. According to documents obtained by Capital, the […]
articles.context: Anbesa Bank, established in 2006, has faced increasing scrutiny amid Ethiopia's evolving financial landscape and political tensions following the 2020 Tigray conflict. The National Bank of Ethiopia's recent intervention reflects broader concerns over governance and accountability in the banking sector, particularly as the government seeks to stabilize the economy and restore investor confidence. The revocation of the board election approval, initially granted in April 2023, underscores the ongoing power struggles within Ethiopia's financial institutions.

articles.title: Gadaa Bank Partners with Top Investment Firms to Roll Out 1-Million Share Issuance
articles.summary: Gadaa Bank S.C. has officially launched a public offering of one million ordinary shares, leveraging strategic partnerships with Ethiopia’s leading investment firms. Following regulatory clearance from the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA), the launch was announced in a joint statement by the bank’s CEO, Wolde Bulto, and Tilahun Esmael, CEO of the Ethiopian Securities Exchange […]
articles.context: Gadaa Bank's share issuance comes at a pivotal moment as Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The Ethiopian government is now focused on economic recovery and attracting investment, making this partnership with top investment firms crucial for rebuilding investor confidence. Additionally, the establishment of the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority in 2021 marks a significant shift towards a more regulated financial environment.

articles.title: Russian foreign minister arrives in Mozambique as part of tour of African countries
articles.summary: Sergey Lavrov has already visited Ethiopia, Niger as part of regional tour which began earlier this week
articles.context: Sergey Lavrov's visit to Mozambique follows his meetings in Ethiopia, where he discussed strengthening ties amid the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region and Ethiopia's strategic position in the Horn of Africa. Russia's increasing engagement in Africa, highlighted by its support for various regimes and involvement in security agreements, reflects a shift in global power dynamics, particularly as Western influence wanes in the region. This tour underscores Russia's intent to bolster its presence in Africa amidst geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: Central Bank Lending Cap Removal Drives 97% Revenue Surge for Hijra Bank
articles.summary: Hijra Bank, which entered Ethiopia’s financial landscape nearly five years ago as a fully interest-free banking institution, has attributed its remarkable success in the 2025/2026 fiscal year to the National Bank of Ethiopia’s (NBE) decision to lift its restrictive lending cap. According to executive leadership, the bank shattered previous records across all Key Performance Indicators […]
articles.context: The removal of the lending cap by the National Bank of Ethiopia in early 2026 comes amid ongoing economic reforms aimed at stabilizing the country post-conflict, particularly following the Tigray War's resolution in late 2022. As Ethiopia seeks to attract investment and bolster its financial sector, Hijra Bank's success reflects both the potential for growth in the interest-free banking sector and the broader economic recovery efforts led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's administration.

articles.title: Sudan's Burhan, African Union envoy discuss peace efforts, reopening of bloc’s liaison office
articles.summary: AU envoy affirms support for ‘Sudan's unity and national sovereignty’
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and the Rapid Support Forces, has resulted in a humanitarian crisis and widespread displacement. The African Union's involvement, particularly through envoy Olusegun Obasanjo, aims to mediate peace efforts and restore stability, especially following the AU's suspension of Sudan's membership in 2021 due to the military coup that ousted Prime Minister Abdalla Hamdok.

articles.title: UK Parliament event highlights Türkiye-UK-Somalia cooperation for Somalia’s future
articles.summary: House of Commons meeting emphasizes strategic partnerships to help Somalia move from humanitarian dependency toward sustainable development
articles.context: The UK Parliament event comes amid ongoing challenges in Somalia, where decades of civil conflict and instability have hindered development. The 2017 New Partnership for Somalia, involving Türkiye, the UK, and Somalia, aimed to foster economic growth and security. With the recent escalation of violence from Al-Shabaab and the humanitarian crisis exacerbated by drought, this meeting underscores urgent international collaboration to transition Somalia from reliance on aid to self-sufficiency.

articles.title: ESX Welcomes Prime Capital as 8th Trading Member
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Securities Exchange (ESX) has reached a new milestone in enhancing the country’s capital market infrastructure. In a ceremony held on the exchange’s trading floor, Prime Capital Investment Bank was officially inducted as the eighth licensed trading member. The addition of this new member expands the market’s ecosystem and strengthens the infrastructure necessary to […]
articles.context: The induction of Prime Capital as the eighth trading member of the Ethiopian Securities Exchange comes amid ongoing efforts to revitalize Ethiopia's economy following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. This expansion of the capital market is crucial as the government seeks to attract foreign investment and stabilize the economy, particularly in light of the recent reforms aimed at liberalizing the financial sector.

articles.title: Sudanese army enters strategic eastern town of Al-Kurmuk after battles with RSF
articles.summary: Military sources tell Anadolu army retook key border town in Blue Nile state from RSF and SPLM-N
articles.context: The town of Al-Kurmuk has been a focal point in the ongoing conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), which escalated following the outbreak of violence in April 2023. The Blue Nile state is strategically important due to its proximity to South Sudan and the presence of the Sudan People's Liberation Movement-North (SPLM-N), complicating the power dynamics in the region amid a backdrop of political instability and humanitarian crises.

articles.title: RSF drone strike kills 10 civilians heading to wedding in central Sudan: Doctors
articles.summary: Sudan Doctors Network says five women from same family among victims, accuses RSF of deliberately targeting civilian vehicle
articles.context: The recent drone strike by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) on civilians in central Sudan highlights the ongoing conflict following the power struggle between the RSF and the Sudanese Armed Forces, which escalated in April 2023. This incident underscores the deteriorating humanitarian situation and the RSF's increasing disregard for civilian safety, exacerbating tensions amid ongoing peace talks aimed at stabilizing the country after years of turmoil and violence.

articles.title: Fears of a massacre in this city on the front line of Sudan's war
articles.summary: El-Obeid has been under "siege-like conditions" for 18 months says the UN human rights chief.
articles.context: El-Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan, has been a focal point in the ongoing conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) since the outbreak of violence in April 2023. The city has faced severe shortages of food and medical supplies due to blockades, exacerbating humanitarian crises. The UN's warning of potential mass atrocities comes amid escalating tensions following the failed Jeddah ceasefire talks in June 2023, highlighting the urgent need for international intervention.

articles.title: ما الذي يحدث مع اللاجئين السودانيين والسوريين في مصر؟
articles.summary: وثقت بي بي سي عدة شهادات لمحتجزين ومرحلين من الجنسيتين السودانية والسورية، وسط تقارير حقوقية ترصد ما وصف بـ"حملة أمنية واسعة" تستهدف اللاجئين وطالبي اللجوء والمهاجرين في مصر على مدار شهري ديسمبر/كانون الأول ويناير/كانون الثاني الماضيين.
articles.context: تشهد مصر منذ ديسمبر 2022 حملة أمنية ضد اللاجئين، تزامناً مع تصاعد الأزمات في السودان وسوريا. في السودان، أدت النزاعات المستمرة بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع إلى نزوح جماعي، بينما يعاني السوريون من تداعيات الحرب الأهلية المستمرة منذ 2011. هذه الظروف تجعل اللاجئين عرضة للاحتجاز والترحيل، مما يثير قلق المنظمات الحقوقية حول حقوق الإنسان في مصر.

articles.title: Outgoing Opposition MP Decries Unresolved Conflicts, Unbearable Cost of Living
articles.summary: Protracted conflict in Tigray, Amhara, Oromia, Gambella, and other parts of the country is inflicting a heavy toll on public safety and national stability, Desalegn Chane (PhD), opposition leader and outgoing MP, said today as the Prime Minister appeared before Parliament to answer questions from lawmakers. Desalegn, a senior figure from the National Movement of […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Ethiopia, particularly since the Tigray War began in November 2020, has exacerbated tensions among various ethnic groups, including the Amhara and Oromia regions. The 2022 Pretoria Agreement aimed to cease hostilities in Tigray, yet violence has persisted in other areas, undermining stability. Desalegn Chane's remarks come amid rising inflation and public discontent, highlighting the urgent need for comprehensive political solutions as Ethiopia approaches critical national elections in 2025.

articles.title: AU and Russia Deepen Ties Ahead of Moscow Summit
articles.summary: The African Union (AU) and Russia have agreed to institutionalize annual high-level political consultations, pledging closer cooperation on security, trade, and global governance ahead of the third Russia–Africa Summit this October in Moscow. The commitment follows high-level talks at the AU Commission headquarters between AUC Chairperson Mahmoud Ali Youssouf and Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov. […]
articles.context: The AU-Russia partnership gains significance amid Ethiopia's ongoing Tigray conflict and the broader geopolitical shifts in the Horn of Africa. Following the 2020 peace agreement between the Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front, regional stability remains fragile. Additionally, Russia's growing influence in Africa, exemplified by its military and economic engagements, positions it as a key player in counterbalancing Western interests, particularly in light of the upcoming Russia–Africa Summit scheduled for October 2023.

articles.title: Arab League warns of looming humanitarian, security catastrophe in Sudan's El-Obeid
articles.summary: Pan-Arab body urges stronger international action to prevent wider crisis in El-Obeid, accuses RSF of targeting civilian infrastructure
articles.context: El-Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan, has been a focal point in the ongoing conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) since the outbreak of violence in April 2023. The RSF's recent assaults on civilian infrastructure have exacerbated the humanitarian crisis, drawing international concern. The Arab League's warning highlights the urgent need for coordinated international efforts to address the deteriorating situation and prevent further destabilization in Sudan.

articles.title: “ENDF Ready to Defend Ethiopia”: Prime Minister
articles.summary: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed (PhD) told lawmakers today the Ethiopian National Defense Force (ENDF) is capable of defending Ethiopia’s sovereignty from internal and external threats. “We know our foreign enemies,” said Abiy, also accusing the TPLF of conspiring with external actors. The PM said TPLF is engaged in forced conscription in Tigray, and sending youth […]
articles.context: The statement comes amid heightened tensions following the resumption of hostilities between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) in late 2020, which led to a devastating civil war. The conflict officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022, but sporadic violence and accusations of TPLF collusion with foreign entities persist, complicating Ethiopia's internal stability and regional security dynamics in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: PM Says Ruling Party Prioritized Inclusive Governance Despite Landslide Election Victory
articles.summary: The incumbent Prosperity Party intentionally did not field candidates in 15 percent of Ethiopia’s electoral constituencies for last month’s elections in a bid to encourage wider opposition participation in governance, according to Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed (PhD). He told lawmakers the ruling party opted earlier this year to carve out space for opposition parties. “A […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant political turmoil since the outbreak of the Tigray conflict in November 2020, which has exacerbated ethnic tensions and led to widespread violence. The Prosperity Party, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has been under scrutiny for its governance approach amid calls for more inclusive politics. The decision to leave 15 percent of electoral constituencies unfilled in the recent elections, held in June 2021, reflects an attempt to address these challenges and promote opposition engagement.

articles.title: Sudanese army says it shot down FH-95 drone in North Kordofan
articles.summary: Military said it intercepted Chinese-made FH-95 drone in North Kordofan, marking 3rd aircraft of same type downed in past 2 weeks
articles.context: The downing of the FH-95 drone comes amid escalating tensions in Sudan, particularly following the outbreak of conflict in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). This incident highlights the ongoing struggle for control and the increasing use of advanced military technology by both sides, raising concerns about the regional implications of foreign arms supplies, particularly from China, in the context of Sudan's fractured political landscape.

articles.title: Drone attacks kill 15 civilians in North Kordofan, Sudanese rights group says
articles.summary: Emergency Lawyers says 2 drone strikes hit civilian vehicles, killing 15 people, including 5 women
articles.context: The recent drone strikes in North Kordofan occur amid escalating violence in Sudan, particularly following the outbreak of conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces in April 2023. This conflict has led to widespread human rights violations and civilian casualties, exacerbating an already dire humanitarian situation. The Emergency Lawyers' report highlights the ongoing disregard for civilian safety, undermining peace efforts initiated by the African Union and the United Nations.

articles.title: "هل خَفُت صوت بريطانيا إزاء ما يحدث في السودان بسبب أموال الإمارات وسوق السلاح؟"- في الفاينانشال تايمز
articles.summary: في عرض الصحف، الثلاثاء، لماذا ينبغي لبريطانيا أن تتحمل مسؤولية إنهاء الحرب في السودان، وكيف حولت الولايات المتحدة خامنئي من "وحش إلى شهيد" في ضوء الجنازة الشعبية المليونية الغاضبة للمرشد الأعلى الإيراني الراحل، وأخيراً تدخل ترامب في كأس العالم أدى إلى "تشويه سمعة اللعبة".
articles.context: تشهد السودان منذ أبريل 2023 صراعًا مسلحًا بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. تتزايد المخاوف من تأثير الإمارات على الصراع، حيث يُعتقد أنها تدعم بعض الأطراف عبر سوق السلاح. في هذا السياق، تُعتبر بريطانيا، التي كانت لها علاقات تاريخية مع السودان، مسؤولة عن الضغط لإنهاء النزاع وتحقيق الاستقرار في المنطقة.

articles.title: Forex Inflow Tops USD 38bln, Says PM
articles.summary: Over USD 38 billion flowed into Ethiopia over the past 12 months, according to the Prime Minister, who praised his administration’s economic reforms before Parliament today. Exports generated USD 11 billion in revenue, with gold and coffee together accounting for USD 8.7 billion, according to the PM. Remittances, foreign direct investment (FDI), and grants made […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been navigating a complex political landscape following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has focused on economic reforms to stabilize the economy and attract foreign investment amidst ongoing challenges, including inflation and regional tensions. The recent influx of foreign exchange is seen as a crucial indicator of recovery and confidence in the administration's policies.

articles.title: Russian foreign minister arrives in Ethiopia on unannounced Africa tour
articles.summary: Russian state media says Sergey Lavrov to hold talks Tuesday with Ethiopian leaders, including his counterpart
articles.context: Sergey Lavrov's visit comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. Russia has been seeking to strengthen its influence in Africa, particularly as Western nations impose sanctions over the Ukraine war. Ethiopia's strategic location and its role in the African Union make it a key partner for Russia in expanding its diplomatic reach on the continent.

articles.title: UN rights council orders investigation into abuses against civilians in Sudan's El-Obeid
articles.summary: UN Human Rights Council tasks fact-finding mission with conducting urgent investigation into violations in El Obeid
articles.context: The UN Human Rights Council's decision comes amid escalating violence in Sudan following the military coup in October 2021, which ousted Prime Minister Abdalla Hamdok. El Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan, has witnessed severe clashes between government forces and armed groups, exacerbating the humanitarian crisis. The investigation aims to address reported abuses linked to the ongoing conflict, particularly as Sudan grapples with political instability and economic turmoil.

articles.title: Gold mine collapse kill 15 workers in northern Sudan
articles.summary: Collapse occurred at Mohamed Tawfiq mine in Northern State
articles.context: The collapse at the Mohamed Tawfiq mine highlights the precarious conditions in Sudan's mining sector, exacerbated by ongoing economic instability and the aftermath of the 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir. With the country grappling with conflict between military factions and armed groups, such as the Sudan People's Liberation Movement-North, safety regulations remain lax, increasing the risk of such tragedies. This incident underscores the urgent need for reform in the mining industry amid a humanitarian crisis.

articles.title: Advertisement Open for LOCAL Procurement
articles.summary: Topic- UNICEF (Ethiopia) wishes to request eligible bidders to participate in an Invitation to Bid (LITB-2026-9204770) 🙂 for the Procurement of Tablets and Laptops. Details of the requirements for this bid and eligibility criteria etc. can be found in the bid document. Interested and eligible bidders from local companies can get the bid document in […]
articles.context: In the wake of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022, Ethiopia faces significant challenges in rebuilding its education and health sectors. The demand for technology, such as tablets and laptops, is critical for enhancing educational access and quality in a country where infrastructure has been severely impacted. UNICEF's procurement initiative underscores the urgent need for local partnerships to support recovery efforts in a post-conflict environment.

articles.title: Tender Notice
articles.summary: The Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR), Representation Office in Ethiopia, Addis Ababa, invites qualified and registered Suppliers to participate in the following tender: SN Tender Subject Closing Date 1 INVITATION TO BID (ITB) UNHCR ITB 3153 Supply and Delivery of Essential Medicines, Medical Supplies, and Medical […]
articles.context: The ongoing humanitarian crisis in Ethiopia, exacerbated by the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020, has led to severe shortages of essential medicines and medical supplies. The UNHCR's tender for these supplies is crucial as it aims to address the urgent health needs of displaced populations, particularly in Tigray and neighboring regions, where access to healthcare remains severely compromised due to ongoing violence and political instability.

articles.title: REQUEST FOR EXPRESSION OF INTEREST (EOI)
articles.summary: This notice is placed by UNECA. The accuracy, reliability and completeness of the contents of furnished information is the responsibility of United Nations Economic Commission for Africa. You are therefore requested to direct all queries regarding this EOI to United Nations Economic Commission for Africa using the fax number or e-mail address provided below. Title […]
articles.context: The United Nations Economic Commission for Africa (UNECA) is seeking expressions of interest amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from a two-year civil conflict that has severely impacted its economy and infrastructure. The Tigray Peace Agreement, signed in November 2022, has opened avenues for international investment and support, making this EOI particularly significant as Ethiopia aims to stabilize and rebuild its economy while fostering regional cooperation in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: UNITED NATIONS DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMME (UNDP)
articles.summary: IC – Individual contractor Brief Consultancy/Job Post Contract Type Procurement Ref. articles.context: The mid-term evaluation of the Strengthening Ethiopia’s Elections for Democracy Sustainability (SEEDS 2) project is critical as Ethiopia navigates a complex political landscape following the 2020 Tigray conflict and ongoing ethnic tensions. articles.title: At least 330 children killed, injured in Sudan in 1st half of 2026: UNICEF
articles.summary: ‘For many children, there is no safe place left,’ says UNICEF representative for Sudan
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has led to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. The fighting has displaced millions and severely impacted children, exacerbating an already dire situation following years of political instability since the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019. The recent surge in casualties among children highlights the urgent need for international intervention and support.

articles.title: UN migration agency warns RSF attacks could turn Sudan’s El Obeid into ‘another El Fasher’
articles.summary: ‘If we do not act now, we will see another El Fasher,’ International Organization for Migration says
articles.context: El Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan, is currently facing escalating violence from the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), a paramilitary group involved in Sudan's ongoing conflict that erupted in April 2023. This violence echoes the tragic events in El Fasher, the capital of North Darfur, where similar RSF actions led to widespread displacement and humanitarian crises. The International Organization for Migration's warning highlights the urgent need for intervention to prevent further destabilization in Sudan.

articles.title: Cholera, measles, food shortages put 200,000 at risk in Sudan's North Kordofan: Medics
articles.summary: Sudan Doctors Network says more than 100 cases of measles recorded among children in Bara town

The region's health infrastructure has deteriorated, exacerbating vulnerabilities to diseases like cholera and measles. The Sudan Doctors Network's report highlights the urgent need for humanitarian assistance amid a backdrop of political instability and food insecurity, with over 200,000 people at risk of severe health crises.

articles.summary: Local relief group reports cholera outbreak across more than 20 villages in state
articles.context: The cholera outbreak in West Kordofan comes amid ongoing instability following the Sudanese military coup in October 2021, which exacerbated humanitarian crises across the country. The Sudanese government’s inability to effectively respond to health crises has heightened the vulnerability of local populations, particularly in rural areas where relief efforts are severely hampered.

articles.title: When preservation becomes distortion
articles.summary: Heritage conservation is often defended in the language of protection. We are told that roofs, shelters, barriers, and “improvements” are necessary to shield fragile monuments from rain, sun, wind, and human contact. Yet the first duty of conservation is not to impose a new idea upon an old site, but to respect the logic of […]
articles.context: In Ethiopia, ongoing tensions related to the Tigray conflict have exacerbated concerns over the preservation of cultural heritage sites, particularly in regions like Tigray, where many historical monuments have suffered damage. The Ethiopian government, alongside international organizations, has initiated restoration efforts, yet these interventions often raise questions about authenticity and respect for local traditions. The 2020-2022 conflict highlighted the need for a balanced approach to conservation that honors historical integrity amidst political upheaval.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s decision to join the New Development Bank: unlocking new avenues for development financing
articles.summary: By Amanuel Tadesse The global development finance architecture is undergoing a profound transformation. Among the most prominent of these institutions is the New Development Bank (NDB), established by the BRICS […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's decision to join the New Development Bank comes amid ongoing economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020. The Ethiopian government, under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, is seeking to rebuild and diversify its economy while addressing the humanitarian crisis resulting from the conflict. Joining the NDB, established by BRICS nations in 2014, offers Ethiopia an opportunity to access alternative financing to support its post-conflict recovery and development initiatives.

As Girmay aims for the green jersey again, his success could inspire a new generation of athletes in a region often overshadowed by conflict.

The recent deaths of several residents in the town of Gimbi have prompted local authorities to enforce drastic measures, including the culling of dogs, raising concerns about public health and animal rights in a country grappling with multiple crises.

articles.title: ماذا تعني كرة القدم للشباب في المنطقة؟
articles.summary: كرة القدم أكثر من مجرد لعبة بالنسبة للكثير من الشباب في منطقة الشرق الأوسط وشمال أفريقيا. فكيف يعبّرون عن شغفهم بها؟ وماذا تعني لهم هذه الرياضة على المستويين الشخصي والاجتماعي؟

articles.title: منظمة العفو تدعو إلى إرسال قوات للحماية في السودان بعد الكشف عن "تطهير عرقي" مع مخاوف من تكراره في الأُبيّض

El-Fasher, the capital of North Darfur, has witnessed severe atrocities, prompting the UN to label the situation as potentially genocidal. The RSF, originally formed from Janjaweed militias, has been implicated in numerous war crimes, raising urgent calls for accountability and justice.

With international organizations like UNICEF actively involved, the need for effective family-based care solutions has never been more urgent.

The ongoing struggle for justice underscores the fragility of Kenya's democratic institutions and the need for reforms.

articles.context: تعيش السودان حالة من التوتر السياسي والاجتماعي منذ الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير في 2019، حيث تتصاعد الاحتجاجات ضد الحكومة الانتقالية بسبب الأزمات الاقتصادية والفساد. في هذا السياق، يواجه المعلمون في ولاية النيل الأبيض ضغوطًا متزايدة، حيث أُوقفوا عن العمل كجزء من إضرابهم للمطالبة بتحسين ظروفهم المعيشية، مما يعكس الصراع المستمر بين السلطة والمواطنين في سعيهم للكرامة والحقوق الأساسية.

articles.title: الأمم المتحدة: السودان لا يزال يشكل أكبر أزمة نزوح عالميًّا
articles.summary: قالت ممثلة المفوضية السامية للأمم المتحدة لشؤون اللاجئين بالسودان، ماري-هيلين فيرني، إن السودان ما زال يشكل أكبر أزمة نزوح عالمياً، في ظل فجوة تواجه تمويل أنشطة المفوضية تصل إلى 72%. وأضافت أن الاحتياجات الأكثر إلحاحاً هي متطلبات حقيقية لإنقاذ الحياة تتركز بشكل كبير في مناطق النزوح الجديد في دارفور وكردفان، وفقا لسودان تربيون..
